summary.narrative: GoWell US Division & Employee Housing comprises two distinct components: (i) a leased 5,200 sqft corporate office suite at 5050 Westway Park Blvd, Suite 100, Houston TX 77041, housing the GoWell Americas business unit (approximately 65 personnel), and (ii) an owned 80-unit employee dormitory complex at 1720 Eldridge Pkwy, Houston TX, acquired in 2019 to address employee housing demand in a high-cost energy corridor. The dormitory currently houses 65 of 80 available units, with subsidised rents of USD 450/month per unit partially offsetting operating costs. The most time-critical issue facing this asset is the Regions Bank facility maturity: the USD 4.1M term loan secured on the dormitory complex matures 15 August 2026, and lender engagement must begin immediately.
summary.keyMessage: CRITICAL: Regions Bank $4.1M term loan matures 15 Aug 2026 — lender outreach must begin by June 2026 · 65/80 dorm units occupied · Houston energy corridor office lease renewed to Oct 2027

basicInfo.assetType: Corporate Office Lease + Employee Dormitory Complex
basicInfo.location: 5050 Westway Park Blvd, Suite 100, Houston TX 77041 (office, leased) | 1720 Eldridge Pkwy, Houston TX 77077 (dormitory, owned)
basicInfo.currentStatus: Operational — 65/80 dorm units occupied, office fully staffed
basicInfo.ownershipSummary: GoWell US Inc. (100% owned); Zhang Family Trust 71.3% beneficial interest through GoWell Energy Solutions Pte. Ltd.

actions.title: Regions Bank Facility Refinancing — URGENT, Loan Matures 15 Aug 2026
actions.recommendation: Initiate formal refinancing process immediately. Contact Regions Bank for extension/rollover terms; simultaneously approach 2–3 alternative lenders (Wells Fargo Real Estate, CBRE Capital Markets, Berkadia). Target: 5-year term, sub-5.50% fixed rate, interest-only period to manage cashflow. If refinancing cannot be secured before August 15, explore bridge financing (6 months) to buy time.
actions.decisionNeeded: Authorise GoWell US Inc. CFO to execute refinancing mandate — board resolution required

actions.title: HVAC Replacement — Building A, West Wing (Phase 2)
actions.recommendation: Building A west wing HVAC units (installed 1997) have exceeded typical 25-year useful life. Phase 1 (east wing) was completed Q4 2024. Phase 2 scope covers 24 units; estimated USD 185,000. Contractor quotations to be obtained before August 2026.
actions.decisionNeeded: Approve Phase 2 CAPEX budget of USD 185,000

actions.title: Office Lease Renewal — 5050 Westway Park Blvd (expires Oct 2027)
actions.recommendation: Begin landlord discussions 12–18 months before lease expiry. The Energy Corridor market has softened (~15% vacancy); GoWell should leverage to negotiate a renewal at 5–8% below current rent or seek concessions (rent-free period, fit-out allowance). Review space requirements given potential headcount growth to 80+ post-IPO.
actions.decisionNeeded: Confirm whether to renew, downsize, or relocate — headcount forecast required

linkedDocuments.name: Regions Bank Facility Agreement — $4.1M Term Loan
Office Lease Agreement — 5050 Westway Park Blvd

topRisks.title: Loan Maturity Default Risk (Aug 2026)
topRisks.impact: Failure to refinance USD 4.1M by 15 Aug 2026 triggers default; potential cross-default on GoWell corporate facilities and SGX IPO disruption.

topRisks.title: Houston Commercial RE Market Softness
topRisks.impact: Energy Corridor vacancy at ~18%; subsidised dorm use limits appraised income — lenders may require lower LTV in refinancing, forcing equity injection.

topRisks.title: HVAC Capital Expenditure Exposure
topRisks.impact: Deferred Phase 2 HVAC replacement increases risk of unit non-habitability and tenant safety complaints during summer months.

marketSnapshot.submarket: Energy Corridor, West Houston — Multi-Unit Residential / Commercial
marketSnapshot.assetVsMarket: Dormitory subsidised at $450/mo vs. market $950/mo — significant below-market occupancy cost for employees
